zh.lifeSummary: Jane Smith於1550年出生在英國素福爾可萊塔姆。她於1573年與Robert Baguley在英國英格蘭素福爾可阿爾德頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1598年在英國英格蘭爾可努恰福德享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可貝德哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Smith于1550年出生在英国素福爾可萊塔姆。她于1573年与Robert Baguley在英国英格兰素福爾可阿爾德頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1598年在英国英格兰爾可努恰福德享年48岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素可貝德哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Jane Smith was born in 1550, in Wrentham, Suffolk, England. She married Robert Baguley in 1573, in Alderton, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1598, in Knutsford, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48, and was buried in Bedham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Smith est née en 1550, à Wrentham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Robert Baguley en 1573, à Alderton, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1598, à Knutsford, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Bedham, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Smith wurde 1550 in Wrentham, Suffolk, England geboren. Sie hat 1573 in Alderton, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Robert Baguley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1598 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Knutsford, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bedham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Smith è nata il nel 1550 nel Wrentham, Suffolk, England. Lei sposato Robert Baguley nel 1573, nel Alderton, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1598, nel Knutsford, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Bedham, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Smithは1550年にイギリススフォルクウレンサムで生まれた。1573年にイギリスイギリススフォルクアルデルトンでRobert Baguleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1598年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアヌトスフォードで48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススセックスベドハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Smith(은)는 1550년 영국 수폴크 렌섬에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1573년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 알더턴에서 Robert Baguley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1598년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 누츠퍼드에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 베드함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Smith nasceu em 1550, em Wrentham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Robert Baguley em 1573, em Alderton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1598, em Knutsford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Bedham, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Smith родилась в 1550 г. в местности рентам, суффолк, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Baguley в 1573 г. в местности алдиртон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1598 г. в местности нуцфорд, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности бедхам, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Smith nació en 1550, en Wrentham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Robert Baguley en 1573, en Alderton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1598, en Knutsford, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Bedham, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
